title: Behavior Tests, Type Tests, and Safe Refactoring · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Evolve a public API while behavior, diagnostics, inferred types, and downstream consumers remain intentionally compatible. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a behavior-and-type regression suite after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.
Write stable rejected-usage tests that fail when diagnostics disappear or move for the wrong reason.
Build changed-schema, invalid-value, async, and clean-consumer fixtures around public boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Behavior Type Tests · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

Which action demonstrates Behavior Type Tests without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests.

content.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Behavior Type Tests condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: The TypeScript Handbook — Controls everyday types, narrowing, functions, objects, generics, classes, modules, and type manipulation sequence.

hints: Name the Behavior Type Tests precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A type test proves the emitted JavaScript behaves correctly.

options.text: Separate runtime behavior tests from compile-time assignability, inference, and expected-error tests. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for a behavior-and-type regression suite.
A type test proves the emitted JavaScript behaves correctly.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Behavior Type Tests evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Behavior Type Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"4" that must not enter the trusted model.

options.text: Declare the domain relationship and identify every untrusted runtime boundary
Infer or annotate the smallest sound type and validate unknown values before narrowing
Run strict diagnostics, inspect emitted JavaScript, and execute the changed case
Add compile-time rejection and runtime boundary evidence, then document remaining limits

Inspect the Type Complexity Restraint trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.title: Type Complexity Restraint case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"5" that must not enter the trusted model.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The most precise possible type is always the best API.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value. Artifact under review: a behavior-and-type regression suite.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Type Complexity Restraint, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Type Complexity Restraint: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A refactor renames retryCount to attempts while retaining runtime behavior. Require a runtime boundary test, a compile-time rejection fixture, and a changed schema with attempts:"6" that must not enter the trusted model.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Replace clever types when diagnostics, performance, or maintenance cost exceeds their safety value.
